title: Lucy dalmatian plush — lower body rebuild and deep clean
description: Lucy's dalmatian plush had worn fabric on the lower body so the tail and legs no longer sat correctly. We opened the skin, rebuilt the lower section, reattached tail and legs, deep-cleaned the plush, and closed the seams so she lies naturally again.
itemDetails:
Lucy dalmatian plush dog is a named dog plush in the classic pet-toy gift segment, commonly sold in toy stores and card/gift outlets from the 1990s onward. Dalmatian styling remains popular because of strong association with family character media and pet themes.

Typical materials include polyester faux fur, fiber fill, and either safety eyes or embroidery for facial details. Value is usually sentimental and condition-led, with stronger pricing only when specific maker tags and original accessories are retained.
repairDetails:
This named dalmatian plush came in with the lower body fabric worn away — the tail and legs needed to be anchored to a sound foundation again.

We opened the skin and removed the lower section, rebuilt the body length to match the remaining fabric, and reattached the tail and leg assemblies. The plush was deep-cleaned while open, then restuffed where needed and stitched closed along the original access seams.
